_purpose: GAMES built from the viz/audio/physics libs -- the library's signature visual IS the core mechanic, with score, high-score (localStorage), escalating levels, win/lose, and a one-more-try loop. Replayable, not dial-slider toys. Run via clean_batch_driver.py.

entries.goal: Build 'Flux Runner', a p5.js arcade game where a living Perlin-noise FLOW-FIELD current IS the mechanic, not a backdrop. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). A grid of flow vectors (visualized as faint drifting streamlines) continuously pushes your little glowing ship; you thrust against or ride the current with arrow keys / WASD / on-screen D-pad + a thrust button to steer through the flow and scoop up energy orbs scattered on the field before your FUEL bar drains. Each orb refills a little fuel and adds score; chaining orbs without hitting a wall builds a COMBO multiplier (x2, x3, x4...) shown with a juicy pop and rising chime. The catch: the flow field's noise seed RESHIFTS at the start of every level so the current you mastered is now fighting you a new way - levels get faster-drifting and more turbulent (higher noise scale + flow strength), with fewer orbs and tighter fuel. WIN a level by collecting all orbs before fuel hits zero; LOSE when fuel empties (ship sputters, screen-shake, particle burst). Score + high-score saved to localStorage with a 'NEW BEST!' flash. Tight 'play again' restart button. Vibe: deep-indigo background, cyan/magenta neon streamlines, particle trail behind the ship, subtle screen-shake on orb pickup and crash. Mobile-friendly with touch D-pad + thrust button; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'Quarantine', a p5.js strategy-arcade game where a live REACTION-DIFFUSION (Gray-Scott) simulation IS the enemy. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). A petri-dish board runs an actual Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion shader/loop spawning an organic spreading bloom from the center; your job is to CONTAIN the outbreak before it touches the dish edge. You have a limited budget of WALL tiles you paint with mouse-drag / finger-drag to block the chemical's spread, plus a few one-shot ANTIDOTE drops that kill a radius of pattern. Score = empty cells saved when the bloom finally stops growing; the slower the spread reaches equilibrium and the more area you save, the bigger the bonus. Escalating LEVELS tune the feed/kill rates so the pattern morphs from gentle blobs to aggressive mitosis-style fingering and coral spirals (each level names its pattern), with a smaller wall budget and faster diffusion. WIN by sealing the bloom away from all four edges; LOSE if the pattern reaches any edge (containment-breach alarm, red flash, screen-shake). High-score saved to localStorage, 'NEW BEST!' banner, instant restart. Vibe: clinical petri-dish on dark steel, biohazard-amber pattern vs cool-blue antidote, satisfying squelchy SFX on wall paint and antidote pop. Mobile-friendly drag controls + on-screen wall/antidote toggle + 'Play Again'; complete on first load.

Build 'Cell Wars', a p5.js territory-capture game where a live VORONOI tessellation IS the board and the scoring. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). The arena is divided into Voronoi cells generated from a set of moving seed points; tap/click an empty cell to drop YOUR seed (claims that cell and recolors it), while a rival AI colony drops its own seeds on a timer. Because the diagram is recomputed live, every new seed RESHAPES the borders of neighboring cells - so a well-placed seed can shrink the enemy's cells and steal area. Score = total area of cells you own (shown as a live percentage bar, you vs AI). You have a limited number of seeds per round and a short timer; spend them to control the most AREA. ESCALATING LEVELS: more aggressive AI, drifting seed points that keep the map fluid, and 'neutral' obstacle seeds that waste space. WIN a round by owning more than 55% area when the timer ends; LOSE if the AI does (defeat flash, screen-shake). Juicy feedback: cells bloom/fill with a ripple when claimed, a 'capture combo' chime when one placement flips multiple borders, particle confetti on round win. High-score (best area %) saved to localStorage with 'NEW BEST!'. Tight 'Play Again' loop. Vibe: glassy stained-glass Voronoi, teal player vs orange AI, neon cell outlines. Mobile-friendly tap-to-place + on-screen seed counter; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'Shepherd', a p5.js herding game where a real BOIDS flocking simulation (separation/alignment/cohesion) IS the gameplay. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). The screen holds a flock of skittish glowing 'sheep' boids that flock and flee; your cursor / finger is a sheepdog whose presence repels nearby boids (an avoidance force). The objective each level: herd the flock into the glowing PEN zone using only your repulsion to nudge the emergent flock - you can never grab a boid, you must exploit how the flocking rules cause the whole herd to swerve together. Score = boids penned, with a TIME BONUS for finishing fast and a STREAK multiplier for penning several in one sweep. ESCALATING LEVELS add more boids, jumpier flee response, predator 'wolf' boids that scatter your flock, and the pen shrinks. WIN by penning the quota before the timer; LOSE on timeout (boids scatter, screen-shake, sad bleat). Juicy: boids leave trails, the pen pulses and chimes as it fills, confetti + fanfare on a full pen, screen-shake when a wolf scatters the herd. High-score saved to localStorage, 'NEW BEST!' flash, instant 'Play Again'. Vibe: dusk-green pasture, warm-amber pen glow, soft white boids, gentle pastoral SFX with a tension sting for the wolf. Mobile-friendly: drag your finger as the dog + on-screen restart; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'Sandfall', a p5.js survival game where a cellular-automata FALLING-SAND simulation IS the threat. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). A real falling-sand / cellular-automata grid pours sand, water, and lava from emitters at the top; you control a tiny miner at the bottom who must SURVIVE and collect glowing gems buried in the cascade. You can place a few DAM blocks (mouse/touch) to divert sand and lava streams away from yourself and toward gem-revealing channels, but blocks are limited and sand piles up and overflows them. Lava kills on contact; water can be safely walked but pushes you; sand buries you if it piles over your head. Score = gems collected; survival TIME adds a steady trickle of points and a multiplier that grows the longer you live. ESCALATING LEVELS open more and faster emitters, add lava, and speed the simulation. WIN by surviving the level timer with the gem quota; LOSE on lava-touch or being buried (ash burst, screen-shake, 'BURIED' / 'MELTED' stamp). High-score (best survival score) saved to localStorage with 'NEW BEST!'. Tight 'Play Again' restart. Vibe: dark cavern, golden sand, glowing orange lava, cool blue water, gem sparkle SFX and a low rumble as piles grow. Mobile-friendly: arrows / on-screen D-pad to move the miner + tap to place dam blocks + restart button; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'Caverunner', a p5.js endless-flyer score-attack where a Perlin-NOISE procedurally-scrolling cave IS the whole level. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). The cave's top and bottom walls are generated each frame from 1D Perlin noise that scrolls toward you, carving an ever-changing winding tunnel that gets narrower and bumpier as you go; you pilot a glowing pod that constantly moves forward, holding thrust (space / tap-and-hold) to rise and releasing to fall with gravity - thread the gap without scraping the noise walls. Score = distance flown, ticking up live; glowing CRYSTAL pickups in the tunnel give bonus points and brief shield, and a 'near-miss' grazing the wall adds a STREAK multiplier with a tension chime. ESCALATING difficulty is baked into the noise: speed ramps, the noise amplitude grows so walls jut more, and the tunnel pinches tighter over distance, with occasional stalactite obstacles. There are no discrete levels - it's pure 'one more run' score-attack with a distance high-score saved to localStorage and a 'NEW BEST!' burst. Instant respawn on crash (wall scrape = explosion, screen-shake, debris particles). Vibe: bioluminescent cave, teal pod with a particle trail, purple crystal glow, pulsing ambient drone that rises with speed. Mobile-friendly: tap-and-hold to thrust + on-screen restart; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'Overgrowth', a p5.js defense game where a procedural L-SYSTEM plant's branching growth IS the enemy. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). A creeping vine grows via a real L-system: each tick the production rules rewrite the string and the plant sprouts new branches that crawl outward from a root, trying to reach and overrun the GLOWING SEEDS you must protect at the screen edges. You hold limited PRUNING SHEARS: click/tap a branch tip to snip it (removing that sub-branch's future growth) and place a few HERBICIDE patches that stop growth in a zone. Because it's an L-system, snipping early near the root prevents a huge cascade of future leaves - strategic timing IS the skill. Score = seeds kept alive + branches pruned efficiently, with a 'clean cut' combo for snipping a tip just before it splits. ESCALATING LEVELS swap in more aggressive production rules (faster branching angle variety, more recursion depth) and more seeds to defend with fewer snips. WIN by keeping at least one seed alive until the plant's growth budget is exhausted; LOSE if the vine reaches every seed (wilting flash, screen-shake). High-score saved to localStorage with 'NEW BEST!'. Tight 'Play Again' loop. Vibe: dark botanical, electric-green vines, golden seed glow, organic snip/squelch SFX and a leafy rustle as the plant grows. Mobile-friendly: tap branch tips to prune + toggle for herbicide + restart; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'Eye of the Storm', a p5.js bullet-hell survival-dodger where a p5.js PARTICLE-SYSTEM storm IS the danger and the score. Load real p5.js via <script src> CDN tag (auto-vendored offline). Thousands of glowing particles emit from rotating spawners around the arena and sweep across the field in physics-driven gusts, spirals, and shockwave rings (real particle forces: gravity wells, vortices, radial bursts); you pilot a tiny ship in the center that must DODGE every particle. The longer you survive untouched, the faster a SURVIVAL multiplier climbs - and grazing close to particles without being hit (a near-miss) charges a COMBO meter that, when full, triggers a screen-clearing SHOCKWAVE pulse you fire (space / tap) for big bonus points and breathing room. Score = survival time x multiplier + graze combos. ESCALATING WAVES introduce new storm patterns every 20 seconds (spiral, cross-fire, expanding rings, gravity-well pull) and ramp particle count and speed; short 'wave cleared' breathers between them. There are no win states beyond surviving waves - it's score-attack 'one more run'; LOSE on any particle hit (ship shatters into particles of its own, big screen-shake, time freeze). High-score saved to localStorage with a 'NEW BEST!' explosion. Instant restart. Vibe: void-black arena, neon-rainbow particle storm, white ship with a graze-glow aura, bass-heavy SFX with a satisfying boom on shockwave. Mobile-friendly: drag to move the ship + tap to fire shockwave + restart; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'PULSE RUNNER', a beat-match survival rhythm game using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.Player/Tone.Transport/Tone.Synth + Tone.Loop scheduling). The CORE MECHANIC is Tone.js's sample-accurate Transport: a steady techno groove (kick on Tone.MembraneSynth, hat on Tone.MetalSynth, bass arpeggio on Tone.MonoSynth) drives glowing beat-orbs that slide down 3-4 lanes locked to the bar grid - you TAP/click the lane button exactly as the orb crosses the hit-line. Tap timing is judged against Tone.Transport.seconds (PERFECT/GOOD/MISS with millisecond windows), each hit fires a Tone.Synth blip and particle burst, and the combo multiplier (x2/x4/x8) climbs with consecutive PERFECTs. You have 3 HEALTH hearts; every MISS or wrong-lane tap costs a heart and stutters the music. ESCALATION: every 16 bars the Transport BPM ramps up (90200) and a 4th lane unlocks, so the orbs come faster - survive as long as you can. SCORE = (hit value x combo), shown live with a juicy bouncing counter; HIGH SCORE persisted to localStorage with a 'NEW RECORD!' screen-shake flash. Tap-to-start to unlock the AudioContext (Tone.start()), clear WIN-by-distance milestone banners every 32 bars, LOSE state when hearts hit 0 with a 'PLAY AGAIN' button that instantly restarts the Transport. Mobile-friendly: big on-screen lane buttons spanning the bottom, touch + keyboard (A/S/D/F) controls. Neon synthwave palette: deep indigo bg, hot-magenta and cyan orbs, gold combo flames. Every button does something; complete and playable on first load.

Build 'ECHO TOWER', a Simon-style melody-memory climber using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.PolySynth/Tone.Sampler + Tone.Sequence for precise playback timing). UNLIKE a plain Simon clone, the CORE MECHANIC is Tone.js polyphonic note scheduling building an ever-growing MUSICAL PHRASE on a pentatonic scale: each round the tower plays back a sequence of glowing pad-notes (5-9 colored pads, each a real pitched Tone.Synth voice with reverb), then YOU must tap them back in order to climb one floor of an animated tower. Get it right and the phrase grows by one note AND the playback tempo speeds up; the melody literally becomes a song you've memorized. SCORING: points per correct note x current floor x a SPEED bonus for tapping quickly; a STREAK meter glows hotter the longer you go. ESCALATION: every 5 floors a new pad/pitch is added to the palette and the playback gets faster and adds a syncopated rhythm, so memorization gets harder. LOSE when you tap a wrong pad (the tower crumbles with a detuned Tone.PitchShift descending tone + screen-shake); WIN-milestone fanfare (Tone.PolySynth chord stab + confetti) every 10 floors. HIGH SCORE (best floor + points) saved to localStorage with a leaderboard-style 'TOP CLIMB' banner. Tap-to-start unlocks audio (Tone.start()), 'PLAY AGAIN' restarts instantly. Mobile-friendly: large finger-sized glowing pads in a fan layout, full touch support, ripple feedback on every tap. Vibe: warm aurora gradient (tealvioletamber), glassy luminous pads, particle sparks on each correct note. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'INTERVAL SNIPER', a fast ear-training quiz GAME using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.PolySynth + Tone.Sampler for piano-ish tones, accurate note triggering). It must be a SCORE-ATTACK arcade game, not a dry quiz: the CORE MECHANIC is Tone.js precise pitch generation - it plays two notes (or a chord) and you must SHOOT the right answer card (e.g. 'Perfect 5th', 'Minor 3rd', 'Major 7th', or for chord mode 'Major/Minor/Dim/Aug') before a shrinking COUNTDOWN ring runs out. Right answer = a satisfying Tone.Synth 'ding', the target shatters into particles, and your STREAK + combo multiplier climbs; wrong answer or timeout = a buzzer (Tone.NoiseSynth) and you lose one of 3 LIVES. ESCALATION: a TEMPO/timer that shrinks each correct answer, and difficulty TIERS that unlock harder intervals (start with octave/5th/4th, then add 3rds/6ths, then tritones/7ths, then full chord-quality mode). Optional 'REPLAY note' button (limited uses, costs combo). SCORE = base x streak-multiplier x speed-bonus, with a live ticking counter; HIGH SCORE + best streak saved to localStorage and celebrated with a 'SHARPSHOOTER!' flash. A daily-ish seeded challenge mode (seed from today's date) gives a fixed question set so players compare runs. Tap-to-start unlocks audio (Tone.start()); LOSE screen at 0 lives with stats and 'PLAY AGAIN'. Mobile-friendly: big tappable answer cards in a grid, on-screen REPLAY button, haptic-style flash feedback. Vibe: dark gunmetal range with neon-green HUD, crosshair reticle, muzzle-flash particles. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'GLIDE CANYON', a continuous pitch 'follow-the-line' game using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.Synth with portamento + Tone.Transport for the scrolling track). The CORE MECHANIC is Tone.js continuous frequency control via setNote/portamento: a sine-wave 'glider' tone whose pitch is bound to the vertical position of your finger/cursor, and you must keep the glider on a winding melodic RIBBON-LINE that scrolls right-to-left through a canyon. While your pitch matches the target line, the tone harmonizes with a backing pad and you bank points + fill a COMBO meter; drift off-pitch and the tone detunes/dissonates (audible feedback) and you stop scoring. The ribbon traces real melodies (so you 'play' a song by tracking it). HEALTH/FUEL bar drains while off-line and refills while on-line; hit canyon walls (line goes off-screen too long) and you crash. ESCALATION: scroll speed and melodic complexity increase over short LEVELS (gentle waves  sharp leaps  trills), each level a named stretch of canyon. SCORE = accuracy time x combo, with a live percent-on-pitch readout; HIGH SCORE + best accuracy saved to localStorage ('CANYON RECORD' banner, screen-shake on new best). Clear-the-level WIN fanfare and a final-distance LOSE screen with 'PLAY AGAIN'. Tap-to-start unlocks audio (Tone.start()). Mobile-friendly: drag anywhere on the canvas (vertical = pitch), works with mouse or touch, on-screen pause button. Vibe: dawn-gradient desert canyon (peachcoralindigo), glowing pitch-ribbon, trailing comet particles behind the glider. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'LOOP FORGE', a step-sequencer PUZZLE game (recreate the target loop) using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.Sequence/Tone.Transport + Tone.MembraneSynth/MetalSynth/MonoSynth for drum+bass voices). UNLIKE a free-play sequencer toy, the CORE MECHANIC is Tone.js step scheduling driving a MATCH-THE-LOOP challenge: each level the game plays a hidden TARGET 16-step beat (kick/snare/hat/bass rows). You then toggle cells on your OWN grid to reconstruct it by ear, can A/B 'Preview yours' vs 'Hear target' a limited number of times, and 'LOCK IN' to score by how many cells match - with a perfect match giving a big bonus and a satisfying Tone.PolySynth chord stab. SCORING: accuracy% x level x a fewer-previews bonus x a faster-solve time bonus; a STREAK of perfect levels multiplies score. ESCALATION: grids start sparse and simple, then add rows, more active steps, syncopation, and tighter preview limits over short LEVELS. LOSE/fail a level if accuracy is below a threshold after lock-in (3 strikes ends the run with a thud); WIN a level with confetti + tempo-rising transition. HIGH SCORE + best streak saved to localStorage ('MASTER FORGER' banner). A daily seeded puzzle (seeded by today's date) so everyone forges the same beat. Tap-to-start unlocks audio (Tone.start()); 'PLAY AGAIN' restarts. Mobile-friendly: chunky tappable grid cells, on-screen PLAY/PREVIEW/LOCK buttons, playing-step highlight sweeps in time. Vibe: industrial neon forge (charcoal bg, molten-orange active cells, cyan playhead), sparks on lock-in. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'SOUND CUE ARENA', an audio-cue reaction game using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.Synth/NoiseSynth/MetalSynth for distinct timbres + Tone.Transport for scheduling). The CORE MECHANIC is Tone.js distinct sound DESIGN as the game signal: the screen shows nothing useful - you must react to DIFFERENT AUDIO CUES, each a recognizable Tone.js timbre mapped to a colored zone/button (e.g. a bright pluck = LEFT, a low thud = RIGHT, a metallic ping = UP, a noise sweep = DODGE). When a cue plays you must tap the matching button as fast as possible; reaction time is measured and converted to points (faster = more), with a hot-streak combo. A wrong tap or too-slow = lose a LIFE (3 lives). ESCALATION: cues come faster, two cues chain together (combo sequences you must answer in order), decoy/feint cues appear, and cue timbres get subtler over short ROUNDS. Juicy feedback: correct = the cue's note plays back an octave up + a particle pop + screen-flash in the zone color; miss = a detuned buzzer + screen-shake. SCORE = sum(speed points) x streak multiplier, live reaction-time readout (avg ms); HIGH SCORE + best average reaction saved to localStorage with a 'LIGHTNING EARS' record flash. Tap-to-start unlocks audio (Tone.start()); LOSE screen with reaction stats and 'PLAY AGAIN'. Mobile-friendly: 4 big colored corner/edge buttons sized for thumbs, full touch, a 'cue legend' tutorial overlay before round 1. Vibe: dark arena with reactive color-burst lighting per cue (electric blue/red/green/amber), shockwave particles. Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'CHORD STACKER', a falling-blocks harmony game using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.PolySynth + Tone.Transport for the drop clock, real chord voicings). The CORE MECHANIC is Tone.js polyphonic chord triggering as the scoring rule: note-blocks (labeled C, E, G, B, etc.) fall down a column grid Tetris-style and you slide/drop them into 3 stacking lanes; whenever a lane's top notes form a RECOGNIZED CHORD (major/minor/7th/triad), Tone.PolySynth plays that chord richly, the notes clear in a shimmering particle burst, and you score - bigger/rarer chords (dominant 7th, diminished) score more. The game literally teaches/rewards harmony through play. A combo multiplier rises with back-to-back chord clears (a 'cadence chain' bonus if you clear chords that form a progression like ii-V-I, with an extra fanfare). LOSE when a lane overflows the top line (board fills up). ESCALATION: blocks fall faster over time and rarer/accidental notes (sharps) enter the bag, demanding trickier voicings; short LEVELS each speed up the Transport. SCORE shown live with a bouncing counter; HIGH SCORE + best combo saved to localStorage with a 'GRAND CADENCE' record screen-shake. Tap-to-start unlocks audio (Tone.start()); 'PLAY AGAIN' restarts instantly. Mobile-friendly: on-screen LEFT/RIGHT/DROP buttons + swipe/tap on lanes, keyboard arrows too. Vibe: glassy music-studio dark theme (deep blue bg, jewel-toned note-blocks color-coded by pitch class, gold chord-clear flares). Complete and playable on first load.

Build 'RHYTHM DASH', an auto-running endless rhythm-platformer using the REAL Tone.js library loaded via a normal <script src> CDN tag (Tone.Transport-driven obstacle spawning + Tone.MembraneSynth/Synth for the beat and SFX). Timing windows give PERFECT/GOOD ratings, a combo multiplier climbs with consecutive on-beat jumps, and a PERFECT chain triggers a 'flow state' where the backing track adds layers (extra synth/hat) and points double. ESCALATION: the Transport BPM and obstacle density rise over distance, double-jump and triplet-rhythm sections unlock as named ZONES. SCORE = distance + (on-beat hits x combo), live counter; HIGH SCORE + best combo saved to localStorage with a 'NEW RECORD' flash. Daily-ish seeded run (seed by date) for fair score comparison. Tap-to-start unlocks audio (Tone.start()); LOSE screen with distance + 'PLAY AGAIN'. Mobile-friendly: tap anywhere to jump, on-screen JUMP button, spacebar on desktop, beat-pulse visual metronome so players feel the grid. Build 'Swarm Shepherd' with pixi.js v7 (load via <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pixi.js/7.4.2/pixi.min.js"></script>). A reverse-tower-defense survival where YOU are a tiny glowing orb and 8,000-10,000 enemy sprites flow toward you as a living swarm rendered in a single PIXI.ParticleContainer (the engine's signature GPU-batched many-sprite capability IS the mechanic: every dot is a real entity with flocking/seek behavior, not a backdrop). OBJECTIVE: survive escalating waves by herding the swarm - your 'shepherd pulse' (tap/click or space) emits a repulsion shockwave that scatters nearby sprites and your trail leaves a slowing pheromone. SCORE = seconds survived x swarm density killed in your pulse rings; combo multiplier for chaining pulses before the ring fades. Every 30s a 'queen' mega-sprite spawns spewing children, raising the live sprite count toward 10k to stress the batcher visibly. Pick ONE upgrade between waves (bigger pulse, faster recharge, lingering pheromone, pierce). High-score to localStorage; clean WIN at wave 10, LOSE on contact. Controls: drag/WASD to move, tap/space to pulse, on-screen pulse + move buttons on mobile. Juicy: additive-blend particle bursts, screen-shake on big pulses, rising synth drone as density climbs. Vibe: bioluminescent deep-sea cyan/magenta on near-black.

Build 'Shatterstorm' with pixi.js v7 (load via <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pixi.js/7.4.2/pixi.min.js"></script>). A score-attack arena shooter where the WHOLE LEVEL is destructible: a wall of thousands of tiny glass-shard sprites (one ParticleContainer, 8k+ shards) that physically shatter, scatter, and fly when hit - pixi's GPU sprite-batching of thousands of independently-moving fragments IS the core mechanic, not decoration. Juicy: glassy crackle SFX, prismatic refraction tint on shards, screen-shake + chromatic flash on barricade collapse. Vibe: neon prism on black, vaporwave palette.

Build 'Polarity' with pixi.js v7 (load via <script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/pixi.js/7.4.2/pixi.min.js"></script>). A one-more-run endless dodger built on MAGNETISM as the mechanic: thousands of charged metal-filing sprites (5k-8k in a ParticleContainer) react in real time to your ship's polarity - pixi's ability to push that many per-frame attract/repel sprite updates on the GPU IS the gameplay, the field literally bends around you. SCORE = distance x core multiplier; near-miss 'graze' bonus when filings whip past you. Speed ramps relentlessly; periodic 'polarity storms' invert controls for tension. Collect cores to bank a one-time SHIELD or MAGNET-BURST. Daily-ish seeded run for a shareable score. High-score to localStorage; pure score-attack LOSE on collision, soft WIN milestones at distance thresholds. Controls: tilt/drag to steer, tap to flip polarity, on-screen flip + steer buttons on mobile. Juicy: filings ripple with field lines, magnetic hum that pitches up with speed, screen-shake on storms. Vibe: clean magnetic blue/red on graphite gray.

A frantic territory-control arcade game where painting IS shooting: you fling expanding ink-bloom blasts made of thousands of additive-blended droplet sprites (ParticleContainer, 8k+ live droplets) that spread and claim the arena - pixi's GPU droplet-batching + a displacement/blur filter on the ink layer IS the mechanic, the bloom physically grows and overtakes enemy paint. SCORE = % territory held x combo for covering enemy paint directly; streak bonus for unbroken coverage gains. Levels are short (45s) with escalating blob counts and tighter target %. Pick a perk per level (bigger blooms, faster spread, a 'splash bomb', droplets that eat enemy ink). High-score (best territory %) to localStorage; WIN by hitting target coverage, LOSE if enemy ink crosses 60%. Controls: aim + tap/hold to fling ink, on-screen aim + fling buttons on mobile. Juicy: wet splat SFX, ink ripples via displacement filter, screen-shake on splash bombs. Vibe: sumi-e meets neon graffiti, ink-black + electric coral/lime.
